stories.title: Ajax swing in Michel's debut to outclass Vojvodina in Conference League
stories.summary: Ajax have started life under manager Michel with a resounding win, beating Serbian side Vojvodina Novi Sad 4-1 in Serbia. Michel was appointed in June after leaving Girona, with whom he was relegated from LaLiga. With the returning Daley Blind as the only new face in Ajax 's starting XI, the Amsterdam outfit started on the front foot through veteran Davy Klaassen , who stood on the receiving end of an inviting Kasper Dolberg cross. Klaassen hit goalkeeper Dragan Rosic with his volley, but tapped in the rebound to get Ajax in front after three minutes.

stories.title: UFC 330 loses Erin Blanchfield vs. Jasmine Jasudavicius
stories.summary: Updated July 23, 2026, 4:12 p. m. ET (Editor's Note: This story has been updated to reflect new information regarding the rescheduling date. ) UFC 330 has lost a contender fight. Erin Blanchfield vs. Jasmine Jasudavicius is off the Aug. 15 event at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia. An injury to Blanchfield (14-2 MMA, 8-1 UFC) has caused the cancellation, with the Jasudavicius (15-4 MMA, 9-3 UFC) matchup expected to be preserved and rescheduled for the UFC Fight Night event Oct. 17 at Rogers Place in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of beating Arsenal to £100m-rated Bradley Barcola transfer
stories.summary: LIVERPOOL are confident of beating Arsenal to £100million-rated Bradley Barcola. The Gunners are eyeing the Paris St Germain and France ace after missing out to Chelsea on the signing of £117m Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. Bradkey Barcola is in-demand this summer Credit: Getty Morgan Rogers signed for Chelsea in a £117m deal Credit: Getty But SunSport understands Liverpool are in the driving seat for Barcola , who is also wanted by Bayern Munich . The Reds are on the hunt for a marquee forward signing after the departure of veteran Mo Salah . Sign up for the Arsenal newsletter Thank you!

stories.title: World Matchplay 2026 results: Luke Littler throws nine-darter in thrilling win over Nathan Aspinall
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler threw a thrilling nine-darter as he booked his place in the quarter-finals of the World Matchplay with an 11-8 win over Nathan Aspinall. Littler began the contest at Winter Gardens in Blackpool in sensational fashion with a perfect leg, finishing on double 15. The Warrington teenager cupped his ears to lap up the adulation of a delirious crowd while fellow Englishman Aspinall laughed in disbelief before he gave Littler a high five. Excitement levels were raised further in the fourth leg when Littler had double 15 remaining for a second nine-darter, only for his arrow to land just the wrong side of the wire.

stories.title: About 150 women turned away for private care by National Maternity Hospital consultants
stories.summary: About 150 women have been turned away from private maternity care at the National Maternity Hospital in Dublin over the past six weeks, as new public-only contracts for consultants take effect. These contracts prohibit doctors from providing private services within public hospitals, leading to a significant reduction in available private care. Experts warn that this trend will worsen in the coming years as more consultants retire and are replaced by those restricted to public-only practice.

stories.title: Bill Paterson back in Wigtown with memoir after nearly 20 years
stories.summary: Bill Paterson brings memoir back to festival after nearly 20 years Image source, Lucy Young Image caption, Tales from the Back Green is being reissued and "slightly updated" Actor Bill Paterson is returning to the Wigtown Book Festival this year - with the same book he brought to the event nearly 20 years ago. His memoir Tales from the Back Green was originally published in 2008 when he first attended the literary gathering in Scotland's national book town and became a patron. Now he is back with a "slightly updated" reissue of the book of tales from his childhood upbringing in 1950s Glasgow.

stories.title: Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero’s Super Limit-Breaking Neo DLC out July 30
stories.summary: Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero made its mark in 2024 as the latest installment of the Budokai Tenkaichi series after 17 years. The game launched with more than 180 characters on day one and currently has over 200 playable characters! However, what is Dragon Ball without surpassing your limits? The Sparking Zero team decided to take it further with the upcoming Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero – Super Limit-Breaking Neo DLC. This new DLC is jam-packed with new characters, stages, a solo mode, and more. The game has several solo-mode options, including Episode Battle, which features branching story paths, the creative Custom Battle mode, and the later-added Survival Mode.

stories.title: Channel 5 viewers say 'fair play Scotland' as Commonwealth Games open
stories.summary: The world looked on as Glasgow took centre stage on Thursday night as the Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony was broadcast live from the city. King Charles and Queen Camilla were the guests of honour for the huge event which took place in the OVO Hydro and promised to honour local culture, humour and identity, featuring local musical headliners KT Tunstall, Nathan Evans and Callum Beattie. Proceedings kicked off with a video sketch showcasing Olympic icon Sir Chris Hoy journeying in the Doctor Who Tardis as he endeavoured to reach Balmoral Castle to collect the King and Queen and escort them to the occasion.

stories.title: Final hurrah? Glasgow’s cut-price Commonwealth Games fight for survival
stories.summary: S ometimes when a non-responsive person is close to death, a strange medical phenomena occurs. From apparently nowhere they experience a burst of energy. They recognise people again. They start to speak in sentences. Relatives even dare to hope. But, sadly, that hope is short-lived. Doctors call it terminal lucidity. And it comes to mind at the dawn of the 23rd Commonwealth Games . Less than three years ago, the Games were on life support after the original hosts, Victoria, effectively paid AUS$380m (£199m) to pull out because it feared losses running into the billions. Glasgow then stepped in to host a cheaper and stripped back event, with only 10 sports compared to 19 four years ago.

stories.title: Intel's stock jumps 11% as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
stories.summary: watch now Intel reported better-than-expected second-quarter results on Thursday, notching its fastest revenue growth rate for any quarter since 2011 and issuing guidance that topped expectations. The stock jumped 11% in extended trading. Here's how the chipmaker did versus LSEG consensus estimates Earnings per share : 42 cents, adjusted, versus 21 cents expected Revenue : $16. 1 billion, versus $14. 42 billion expected Intel shares are up over 170% so far in 2026 as of Thursday's close after soaring 84% last year, when the U. S. government took a 10% stake in the company as part of an effort to support U. S. chip manufacturing.

stories.title: You've Been Saying Scarlett Johansson's Name Wrong This Entire Time
stories.summary: by Larry Fitzmaurice BuzzFeed Staff I'm a writer and editor who lives in Brooklyn, New York, and I've written for a variety of publications across the last 20 years, including The New York Times, The Guardian, GQ, New York Magazine, Pitchfork, The Fader—the list goes on.

stories.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
stories.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: The Rebuild on Tyneside: Restoring Newcastle’s Identity
stories.summary: Newcastle United spent last season looking like a club that had forgotten its own blueprint. The relentless, suffocating press that initially defined Eddie Howe’s revolution withered away into a passive, mid-table slog characterised by defensive regression and a lack of squad cohesion. The departures of Alexander Isak previously, followed by Anthony Gordon’s exit to Barcelona and Sandro Tonali’s £100m move to Tottenham, left a squad starved of identity and bleeding goals. If St James’ Park is to become a fortress again in the upcoming campaign, the Magpies must transition from a team in a state of chaotic transition.

stories.title: Sabrina Ionescu puts on unreal scoring barrage, leads Liberty to win
stories.summary: July 23, 2026, 12:58 p. m. PT With 58. 9 seconds remaining on the clock, the New York Liberty found themselves down eight points to the Chicago Sky on Wednesday night. The game was all but over, with Chicago seemingly snapping New York's two-game winning streak. But the Liberty had Sabrina Ionescu on their side. The former Oregon Duck tossed in an impressive, fading three-pointer from the right corner to bring the Liberty to within five with 55 seconds remaining. After New York got a defensive stop, she nailed a top-of-the-key, off balance triple with under 30 seconds left. Two-point game. The Sky then went 1-of-2 on free throws, bringing their lead to three points.

stories.title: The Hundred 2026 results: Manchester Super Giants thrash dismal London Spirit at Lord's
stories.summary: The Hundred women's competition, Lord's London Spirit 80 all out (91 balls) : Kapp 17 (12); MacDonald-Gay 4-17 Manchester Super Giants 86-3 (59 balls) : Scholfield 22 (15); Kapp 2-18 Manchester Super Giants won by seven wickets Scorecard . Table Manchester Super Giants chased a target of 81 in just 59 balls as they thrashed London Spirit by seven wickets in The Hundred. Ryana MacDonald-Gay took 4-17 as Super Giants bowled out Spirit with nine balls to spare at Lord's, before the visiting batters completed their chase for the loss of just three wickets.

stories.title: Italy In Talks With Pep Guardiola, Carlo Ancelotti To Coach National Team
stories.summary: The Italian soccer federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup. Former Italy and AC Milan defender Paolo Maldini was named as the federation’s new technical director at the start of the month and was present at a meeting with the Serie A clubs on Thursday, along with new FIGC president Giovanni Malagò and special advisor Leonardo. "Honestly, today we can’t give news about what’s happening.

stories.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
stories.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

stories.title: WilCo Precinct 2 Chief Deputy James Thomas and Sgt. Guy Lanford earn FBI-LEEDA Trilogy Award
stories.summary: NewsFlash News Flash provides the latest community news, announcements, and updates. Browse recent articles or use the category filters to view news related to specific topics. Stay informed by clicking Notify Me® to subscribe and receive automatic notifications when new news items are published. Don’t Drown. Do not attempt to drive or walk through moving water over low water crossings.

stories.title: 'The Lost Children of Tuam' Review: A Stirring Irish Reckoning
stories.summary: Ireland’s ongoing artistic reckoning with the Catholic Church’s gross mistreatment of unmarried mothers and their children — as addressed in such films as “The Magdalene Sisters,” “Philomena” and “Small Things Like These” — continues in “The Lost Children of Tuam.

stories.title: Access Mammoth, Wild Iris: ‘Transforming Loss into Legacy’
stories.summary: Featured Alvarado-Gil announces Mono, Inyo Nonprofits of the Year Mammoth Times Staff Jul 23, 2026 1 hr ago Comments Members of the Mammoth Lakes Town Council pose with Access Mammoth staff as the nonprofit is lauded with Sen. Marie Alvarado-Gil’s 2026 Mono County Nonprofit of the Year Award. Marie Alvarado-Gil. Marie Alvarado-Gil/Courtesy California State Sen.

stories.title: Epstein survivor urges Senate Democrats to block Blanche confirmation
stories.summary: An Epstein sex trafficking survivor urged Democratic senators on Capitol Hill to vote no on the confirmation of Todd Blanche as U. S. attorney general.

stories.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment
stories.summary: As the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr has authority over the nation’s TV, radio, and internet. But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has wielded that power to threaten broadcasters that have exercised their free speech rights to make jokes about the president. He was even able to get ABC to briefly pull Jimmy Kimmel from his late-night show. As The Verge editor-in-chief Nilay Patel has said, Carr’s mission is to “trash the First Amendment. ” We’ll be following closely as the FCC chairman attempts to erode Americans’ constitutional rights.

stories.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
stories.summary: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319. 99 (usually $399. 99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. [Product: Amazon Echo Show 21 | https://bestbuy. 7tiv. net/c/482924/614286/10014? u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww. bestbuy. com%2Fproduct%2Famazon-echo-show-21-full-hd-21-smart-display-for-home-organization-with-built-in-fire-tv-and-alexa-black%2FJ39HW6R5LZ, https://go. skimresources. com/?

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

stories.title: Beach Boys Call Off Remainder of Summer Concerts ‘to Recharge’ Before Resuming in Fall
stories.summary: The Beach Boys ‘ endless summer has run into a hitch. The touring version of the group, led by sole remaining original member Mike Love , has called off six weeks’ worth of concerts scheduled for this summer, amounting to nearly 30 dates, citing the need to “recharge” before hitting the road again in the fall. The cancellations and postponements cover the concerts that the band had scheduled from Thursday night, when they were to have played at Wolf Trap in Virginia, through Sept. 3, when they were scheduled to perform at the Colorado State Fair.

stories.title: Wales at the Commonwealth Games
stories.summary: Image source, BBC Sport Maddy Connelly has withdrawn from the 2026 Commonwealth Games because of injury. Connelly was one of seven weightlifters included in Team Wales for the showpiece event in Glasgow. But the 2025 British champion - who was due to compete in the women's 53kg category - has confirmed she sustained an injury in training, forcing her to pull out of the Games. "To say I'm disappointed is an understatement," she wrote on Instagram. "Representing Team Wales on the Commonwealth stage has been a huge dream of mine and it's heart-breaking not to be lining up with my team-mates in Glasgow and fighting for that place on the podium. "Right now, my focus is on recovery.

stories.title: Matchday-1: Training in Amsterdam before departure for Serbia
stories.summary: Ajax's competitive campaign officially gets under way. On Thursday evening at 20:00, the Amsterdammers will face FK Vojvodina away in the second qualifying round of the UEFA Conference League. Before the squad departed for Serbia, the group trained in Amsterdam on Wednesday. Date: 22. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles Inspired by Ajax Originals – 26/27 away kit This is the new away kit for the 2026/2027 season. The design of the kit is inspired by nine iconic moments in Ajax history, including winning the third star in 2011, the UEFA Champions League triumph in 1995 and the European Cup I trilogy in 1971, 1972 and 1973.

stories.title: ‘Avatar: Seven Havens’ Sets 2026 Release Date at Paramount+, Drops Apocalyptic First Teaser
stories.summary: “ Avatar: Seven Havens ” has an official premiere date at Paramount+ . And what a date it is. The new entry in the “Avatar” franchise will debut on Oct. 9 on the streaming service. That date should stick out to fans, as it was originally the date set for the premiere of the movie “Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender,” before that release was moved up to July 25 after the movie leaked online earlier this year. And unlike past “Avatar” shows, which released weekly and had time between seasons, all 26 episodes of “Seven Havens” (divided into two “books” as with the past shows) will debut all at once as a binge release.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region. Local officials said some 3,400 hectares (8,400 acres) of land have already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon, as about 700 firefighters battle the flames. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula. Wildfires have also recently broken out in Spain and Italy.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

stories.title: Help for electricity bills as crude oil hits $100
stories.summary: The States of Guernsey said it would give Alderney Electricity £200,000 to offset islanders' bills. Jeannie Cameron, who chairs the committee, said the funds would be applied directly to customers' bills. She said: "The recent rise in global oil prices has the potential to place additional financial pressure on households and businesses at a time when many are already managing increased costs. "This temporary measure provides a level of protection against exceptional fuel price increases while ensuring that public funds are only used where they are genuinely needed. " Cameron said unlike its sister islands Guernsey and Jersey, Alderney did not have cable supplying electricity from France.

stories.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
stories.summary: Some hospitality firms will get a discount from next April but hotels and restaurants won't get a cut. By Henry Zeffman Chief political correspondent and Archie Mitchell Business reporter Pubs, social clubs and live music venues in England will be given a 20% cut to business rates from April, with new Prime Minister Andy Burnham calling it a "first step" to help the industry. It is his third policy announcement since entering No 10, and the government estimates the plan will save firms around £1,100 next year. With a cost of £100m, it will be funded by a review of tax relief on firms such as vape shops which "do not make a positive contribution to local communities", the government said.

stories.title: HNK Rijeka Away – Live Stream
stories.summary: We are delighted to provide details of a Live Stream for Thursday’s UEFA Conference League away leg against HNK Rijeka in Croatia. Partnering with Solidsport , the second qualifying round game at Stadion HNK Rijeka will be broadcast live on derrycityplay. com costing £12. Stream Link Commentary is due to be provided by Drive 105. The match will kick-off at 7. 45pm Irish time , 8. 45pm local. The Brunswick Moviebowl are also showing the game live in their cinema. Book Your Cinema Tickets here.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: 2 min read Jessica Taveau Jul 23, 2026 RELEASE 26-059 Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. Fire TV customers can easily access this content by asking Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future programming on Fire TV may include science mission launches, a test flight for rendezvous and docking with human landing systems, robotic lunar deliveries, the first crewed mission to the Moon under Artemis, and more. As always, NASA+ also remains available for free, with no ads, through the NASA app and on the agency’s website .

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic’s faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering answers to quick questions with minimal delay. But in a blog post, the company said people immediately started using voice mode for far more than casual queries. They were using it to work “through real business problems,” which Haiku was not really designed for. That model “kept conversations quick, but not always deep.

stories.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
stories.summary: Italy held discussions with Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before opening talks with Pep Guardiola. Italy approached Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before holding talks with Pep Guardiola. The Azzurri have been without a head coach since Gennaro Gattuso resigned in April following the country's failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup. Earlier this week, Italian Football Federation (FIGC) president Giovanni Malago said talks had been held with Guardiola, who left Manchester City this summer after 10 years, and "exceptions" could be made for his salary.

stories.title: Diana Morant unleashes a storm by comparing Feijóo's direction to Hitler: PP demands her resignation and PSOE distances itself
stories.summary: Diana Morant has caused one of the biggest political controversies this summer by comparing the path taken by Alberto Núñez Feijóo alongside the far-right Vox with the "criminal governments" of Adolf Hitler . The PP began by demanding a rectification and this Thursday has escalated its response to demanding the immediate resignation of the Minister of Science and leader of the PSPV-PSOE. The response has also reached the socialist ranks. Patxi López has clearly distanced himself from Morant's words , while Sumar and Podemos have questioned the use of such an extreme historical reference .

stories.title: One Avengers: Doomsday Trailer Detail May Confirm Sam Raimi's Spider-Man Rumor
stories.summary: Primary Subject: Avengers: DoomsdayKey Update: Unconfirmed trailer details and insider leaks hint that Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men universe may face a fatal Incursion event. Status: Speculation / RumorsLast Verified: July 23, 2026Quick Answer: Rumors suggest Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men collide during an Incursion event in Avengers: Doomsday, leading to tragic character deaths. The Avengers: Doomsday trailer is packed with hidden hints and clues that may predict the movie's future, including a possible connection to Sam Raimi's original Spider-Man trilogy.

stories.title: U.S. to Withdraw Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times said were an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Clint Reilly, Political Consultant Known for Feuding, Dies at 79
stories.summary: As savvy as he could be volatile, he advised Nancy Pelosi and Dianne Feinstein, ran for mayor of San Francisco and bought The San Francisco Examiner.

stories.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
stories.summary: AC Milan midfielder Luka Modric - who turns 41 in September - signs a one-year contract extension at the club. AC Milan midfielder Luka Modric - who turns 41 in September - has signed a one-year contract extension. The Croatian joined Milan last summer on a one-year deal with an option to extend after leaving Real Madrid. After 13 years at the Bernabeu, Modric scored two goals in 37 appearances for Milan in a season when they finished fifth in Serie A and only reached the last-16 stage of the Coppa Italia. "I am thrilled to stay at AC Milan, where you made me feel welcomed and loved from the very first day," Modric said of his new deal.

stories.title: Sumar deems Zapatero's explanations on TVE insufficient and demands he clarify the origin of the jewelry and payments to his daughters
stories.summary: The explanations offered this Thursday by José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on ‘Mañaneros 360’ have not convinced Sumar. The minority partner of the Government has considered his answers insufficient regarding the ‘Plus Ultra case’ and has demanded that he clarify both the origin of the jewels found in his office and the payments received by his daughters' company. Sources from the party have indicated that the former president has the right to defend his innocence, but have added that he "continues to disappoint" and that his intervention on TVE has not been up to the explanations expected by a part of the progressive electorate.

stories.title: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
stories.summary: Also, the full trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil features a likable everyman. Coyote vs. Acme hits theaters on August 28, 2026. Resident Evil The Resident Evil film franchise has grossed over $1. 2 billion worldwide since the first film debuted in 2002, but an attempt to reboot it a few years ago floundered. Sony Pictures is trying again, this time tapping Zach Cregger—who wrote, produced, and directed last year’s Oscar-winning horror hit Weapons —for the project. The studio showed the first teaser for Cregger’s Resident Evil during CinemaCon in April and dropped the full trailer today at SDCC.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: Long-term exposure to road traffic noise is associated with a higher risk of Parkinson’s disease, the biggest study of its kind has found. Parkinson’s disease is a progressive disorder in which parts of the brain become damaged over time, affecting movement and balance. Existing research has found potential biological pathways that link noise exposure to Parkinson’s disease, largely due to stress responses and disrupted sleep. The researchers modelled noise exposure at the most and least exposed exterior of the residence of each participant and calculated the difference in noise levels. The magnitude of the effect was modest but consistent; at the most exposed facade, for every 11.

offbeat.title: Ajax swing in Michel's debut to outclass Vojvodina in Conference League
offbeat.summary: Ajax have started life under manager Michel with a resounding win, beating Serbian side Vojvodina Novi Sad 4-1 in Serbia. Michel was appointed in June after leaving Girona, with whom he was relegated from LaLiga. With the returning Daley Blind as the only new face in Ajax 's starting XI, the Amsterdam outfit started on the front foot through veteran Davy Klaassen , who stood on the receiving end of an inviting Kasper Dolberg cross. Klaassen hit goalkeeper Dragan Rosic with his volley, but tapped in the rebound to get Ajax in front after three minutes.

offbeat.title: UFC 330 loses Erin Blanchfield vs. Jasmine Jasudavicius
offbeat.summary: Updated July 23, 2026, 4:12 p. m. ET (Editor's Note: This story has been updated to reflect new information regarding the rescheduling date. ) UFC 330 has lost a contender fight. Erin Blanchfield vs. Jasmine Jasudavicius is off the Aug. 15 event at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia. An injury to Blanchfield (14-2 MMA, 8-1 UFC) has caused the cancellation, with the Jasudavicius (15-4 MMA, 9-3 UFC) matchup expected to be preserved and rescheduled for the UFC Fight Night event Oct. 17 at Rogers Place in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada.

offbeat.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
offbeat.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

offbeat.title: Liverpool confident of beating Arsenal to £100m-rated Bradley Barcola transfer
offbeat.summary: LIVERPOOL are confident of beating Arsenal to £100million-rated Bradley Barcola. The Gunners are eyeing the Paris St Germain and France ace after missing out to Chelsea on the signing of £117m Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. Bradkey Barcola is in-demand this summer Credit: Getty Morgan Rogers signed for Chelsea in a £117m deal Credit: Getty But SunSport understands Liverpool are in the driving seat for Barcola , who is also wanted by Bayern Munich . The Reds are on the hunt for a marquee forward signing after the departure of veteran Mo Salah . Sign up for the Arsenal newsletter Thank you!

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026 results: Luke Littler throws nine-darter in thrilling win over Nathan Aspinall
offbeat.summary: World number one Luke Littler threw a thrilling nine-darter as he booked his place in the quarter-finals of the World Matchplay with an 11-8 win over Nathan Aspinall. Littler began the contest at Winter Gardens in Blackpool in sensational fashion with a perfect leg, finishing on double 15. The Warrington teenager cupped his ears to lap up the adulation of a delirious crowd while fellow Englishman Aspinall laughed in disbelief before he gave Littler a high five. Excitement levels were raised further in the fourth leg when Littler had double 15 remaining for a second nine-darter, only for his arrow to land just the wrong side of the wire.

offbeat.title: About 150 women turned away for private care by National Maternity Hospital consultants
offbeat.summary: About 150 women have been turned away from private maternity care at the National Maternity Hospital in Dublin over the past six weeks, as new public-only contracts for consultants take effect. These contracts prohibit doctors from providing private services within public hospitals, leading to a significant reduction in available private care. Experts warn that this trend will worsen in the coming years as more consultants retire and are replaced by those restricted to public-only practice.

offbeat.title: Bill Paterson back in Wigtown with memoir after nearly 20 years
offbeat.summary: Bill Paterson brings memoir back to festival after nearly 20 years Image source, Lucy Young Image caption, Tales from the Back Green is being reissued and "slightly updated" Actor Bill Paterson is returning to the Wigtown Book Festival this year - with the same book he brought to the event nearly 20 years ago. His memoir Tales from the Back Green was originally published in 2008 when he first attended the literary gathering in Scotland's national book town and became a patron. Now he is back with a "slightly updated" reissue of the book of tales from his childhood upbringing in 1950s Glasgow.

offbeat.title: Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero’s Super Limit-Breaking Neo DLC out July 30
offbeat.summary: Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero made its mark in 2024 as the latest installment of the Budokai Tenkaichi series after 17 years. The game launched with more than 180 characters on day one and currently has over 200 playable characters! However, what is Dragon Ball without surpassing your limits? The Sparking Zero team decided to take it further with the upcoming Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero – Super Limit-Breaking Neo DLC. This new DLC is jam-packed with new characters, stages, a solo mode, and more. The game has several solo-mode options, including Episode Battle, which features branching story paths, the creative Custom Battle mode, and the later-added Survival Mode.

offbeat.title: Channel 5 viewers say 'fair play Scotland' as Commonwealth Games open
offbeat.summary: The world looked on as Glasgow took centre stage on Thursday night as the Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony was broadcast live from the city. King Charles and Queen Camilla were the guests of honour for the huge event which took place in the OVO Hydro and promised to honour local culture, humour and identity, featuring local musical headliners KT Tunstall, Nathan Evans and Callum Beattie. Proceedings kicked off with a video sketch showcasing Olympic icon Sir Chris Hoy journeying in the Doctor Who Tardis as he endeavoured to reach Balmoral Castle to collect the King and Queen and escort them to the occasion.

offbeat.title: Final hurrah? Glasgow’s cut-price Commonwealth Games fight for survival
offbeat.summary: S ometimes when a non-responsive person is close to death, a strange medical phenomena occurs. From apparently nowhere they experience a burst of energy. They recognise people again. They start to speak in sentences. Relatives even dare to hope. But, sadly, that hope is short-lived. Doctors call it terminal lucidity. And it comes to mind at the dawn of the 23rd Commonwealth Games . Less than three years ago, the Games were on life support after the original hosts, Victoria, effectively paid AUS$380m (£199m) to pull out because it feared losses running into the billions. Glasgow then stepped in to host a cheaper and stripped back event, with only 10 sports compared to 19 four years ago.

offbeat.title: Intel's stock jumps 11% as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
offbeat.summary: watch now Intel reported better-than-expected second-quarter results on Thursday, notching its fastest revenue growth rate for any quarter since 2011 and issuing guidance that topped expectations. The stock jumped 11% in extended trading. Here's how the chipmaker did versus LSEG consensus estimates Earnings per share : 42 cents, adjusted, versus 21 cents expected Revenue : $16. 1 billion, versus $14. 42 billion expected Intel shares are up over 170% so far in 2026 as of Thursday's close after soaring 84% last year, when the U. S. government took a 10% stake in the company as part of an effort to support U. S. chip manufacturing.

offbeat.title: You've Been Saying Scarlett Johansson's Name Wrong This Entire Time
offbeat.summary: by Larry Fitzmaurice BuzzFeed Staff I'm a writer and editor who lives in Brooklyn, New York, and I've written for a variety of publications across the last 20 years, including The New York Times, The Guardian, GQ, New York Magazine, Pitchfork, The Fader—the list goes on.

offbeat.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
offbeat.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: The Rebuild on Tyneside: Restoring Newcastle’s Identity
offbeat.summary: Newcastle United spent last season looking like a club that had forgotten its own blueprint. The relentless, suffocating press that initially defined Eddie Howe’s revolution withered away into a passive, mid-table slog characterised by defensive regression and a lack of squad cohesion. The departures of Alexander Isak previously, followed by Anthony Gordon’s exit to Barcelona and Sandro Tonali’s £100m move to Tottenham, left a squad starved of identity and bleeding goals. If St James’ Park is to become a fortress again in the upcoming campaign, the Magpies must transition from a team in a state of chaotic transition.

offbeat.title: Sabrina Ionescu puts on unreal scoring barrage, leads Liberty to win
offbeat.summary: July 23, 2026, 12:58 p. m. PT With 58. 9 seconds remaining on the clock, the New York Liberty found themselves down eight points to the Chicago Sky on Wednesday night. The game was all but over, with Chicago seemingly snapping New York's two-game winning streak. But the Liberty had Sabrina Ionescu on their side. The former Oregon Duck tossed in an impressive, fading three-pointer from the right corner to bring the Liberty to within five with 55 seconds remaining. After New York got a defensive stop, she nailed a top-of-the-key, off balance triple with under 30 seconds left. Two-point game. The Sky then went 1-of-2 on free throws, bringing their lead to three points.

offbeat.title: The Hundred 2026 results: Manchester Super Giants thrash dismal London Spirit at Lord's
offbeat.summary: The Hundred women's competition, Lord's London Spirit 80 all out (91 balls) : Kapp 17 (12); MacDonald-Gay 4-17 Manchester Super Giants 86-3 (59 balls) : Scholfield 22 (15); Kapp 2-18 Manchester Super Giants won by seven wickets Scorecard . Table Manchester Super Giants chased a target of 81 in just 59 balls as they thrashed London Spirit by seven wickets in The Hundred. Ryana MacDonald-Gay took 4-17 as Super Giants bowled out Spirit with nine balls to spare at Lord's, before the visiting batters completed their chase for the loss of just three wickets.

offbeat.title: Italy In Talks With Pep Guardiola, Carlo Ancelotti To Coach National Team
offbeat.summary: The Italian soccer federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup. Former Italy and AC Milan defender Paolo Maldini was named as the federation’s new technical director at the start of the month and was present at a meeting with the Serie A clubs on Thursday, along with new FIGC president Giovanni Malagò and special advisor Leonardo. "Honestly, today we can’t give news about what’s happening.

offbeat.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
offbeat.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

offbeat.title: WilCo Precinct 2 Chief Deputy James Thomas and Sgt. Guy Lanford earn FBI-LEEDA Trilogy Award
offbeat.summary: NewsFlash News Flash provides the latest community news, announcements, and updates. Browse recent articles or use the category filters to view news related to specific topics. Stay informed by clicking Notify Me® to subscribe and receive automatic notifications when new news items are published. Commissioner Cook Shares Severe Weather Resources Severe weather, including flash floods and tornadoes, is becoming more and more the norm, and Williamson County has experienced all of it. · Turn around. Don’t Drown. Do not attempt to drive or walk through moving water over low water crossings.

offbeat.title: 'The Lost Children of Tuam' Review: A Stirring Irish Reckoning
offbeat.summary: Ireland’s ongoing artistic reckoning with the Catholic Church’s gross mistreatment of unmarried mothers and their children — as addressed in such films as “The Magdalene Sisters,” “Philomena” and “Small Things Like These” — continues in “The Lost Children of Tuam.

offbeat.title: Access Mammoth, Wild Iris: ‘Transforming Loss into Legacy’
offbeat.summary: Featured Alvarado-Gil announces Mono, Inyo Nonprofits of the Year Mammoth Times Staff Jul 23, 2026 1 hr ago Comments Members of the Mammoth Lakes Town Council pose with Access Mammoth staff as the nonprofit is lauded with Sen. Marie Alvarado-Gil’s 2026 Mono County Nonprofit of the Year Award. Access Mammoth/Courtesy Dominic Hays, executive director of Wild Iris Family Counseling & Crisis Center, surrounded by Inyo County Supervisors, holds up the 2026 Inyo County Nonprofit of the Year Award that Wild Iris received from State Sen. Marie Alvarado-Gil. Marie Alvarado-Gil/Courtesy California State Sen.

offbeat.title: Epstein survivor urges Senate Democrats to block Blanche confirmation
offbeat.summary: An Epstein sex trafficking survivor urged Democratic senators on Capitol Hill to vote no on the confirmation of Todd Blanche as U. S. attorney general.

offbeat.title: Wales at the Commonwealth Games
offbeat.summary: Image source, BBC Sport Maddy Connelly has withdrawn from the 2026 Commonwealth Games because of injury. Connelly was one of seven weightlifters included in Team Wales for the showpiece event in Glasgow. But the 2025 British champion - who was due to compete in the women's 53kg category - has confirmed she sustained an injury in training, forcing her to pull out of the Games. "To say I'm disappointed is an understatement," she wrote on Instagram. "Representing Team Wales on the Commonwealth stage has been a huge dream of mine and it's heart-breaking not to be lining up with my team-mates in Glasgow and fighting for that place on the podium. "Right now, my focus is on recovery.

offbeat.title: Matchday-1: Training in Amsterdam before departure for Serbia
offbeat.summary: Ajax's competitive campaign officially gets under way. On Thursday evening at 20:00, the Amsterdammers will face FK Vojvodina away in the second qualifying round of the UEFA Conference League. Before the squad departed for Serbia, the group trained in Amsterdam on Wednesday. Date: 22. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles Inspired by Ajax Originals – 26/27 away kit This is the new away kit for the 2026/2027 season. The design of the kit is inspired by nine iconic moments in Ajax history, including winning the third star in 2011, the UEFA Champions League triumph in 1995 and the European Cup I trilogy in 1971, 1972 and 1973.

offbeat.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
offbeat.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region. Local officials said some 3,400 hectares (8,400 acres) of land have already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon, as about 700 firefighters battle the flames. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula. Wildfires have also recently broken out in Spain and Italy.

offbeat.title: HNK Rijeka Away – Live Stream
offbeat.summary: We are delighted to provide details of a Live Stream for Thursday’s UEFA Conference League away leg against HNK Rijeka in Croatia. Partnering with Solidsport , the second qualifying round game at Stadion HNK Rijeka will be broadcast live on derrycityplay. com costing £12. Stream Link Commentary is due to be provided by Drive 105. The match will kick-off at 7. 45pm Irish time , 8. 45pm local. The Brunswick Moviebowl are also showing the game live in their cinema. Book Your Cinema Tickets here.

offbeat.title: Diana Morant unleashes a storm by comparing Feijóo's direction to Hitler: PP demands her resignation and PSOE distances itself
offbeat.summary: Diana Morant has caused one of the biggest political controversies this summer by comparing the path taken by Alberto Núñez Feijóo alongside the far-right Vox with the "criminal governments" of Adolf Hitler . The PP began by demanding a rectification and this Thursday has escalated its response to demanding the immediate resignation of the Minister of Science and leader of the PSPV-PSOE. The response has also reached the socialist ranks. Patxi López has clearly distanced himself from Morant's words , while Sumar and Podemos have questioned the use of such an extreme historical reference .

offbeat.title: One Avengers: Doomsday Trailer Detail May Confirm Sam Raimi's Spider-Man Rumor
offbeat.summary: Primary Subject: Avengers: DoomsdayKey Update: Unconfirmed trailer details and insider leaks hint that Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men universe may face a fatal Incursion event. Status: Speculation / RumorsLast Verified: July 23, 2026Quick Answer: Rumors suggest Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and Fox's X-Men collide during an Incursion event in Avengers: Doomsday, leading to tragic character deaths. The Avengers: Doomsday trailer is packed with hidden hints and clues that may predict the movie's future, including a possible connection to Sam Raimi's original Spider-Man trilogy.

offbeat.title: Sumar deems Zapatero's explanations on TVE insufficient and demands he clarify the origin of the jewelry and payments to his daughters
offbeat.summary: The explanations offered this Thursday by José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on ‘Mañaneros 360’ have not convinced Sumar. The minority partner of the Government has considered his answers insufficient regarding the ‘Plus Ultra case’ and has demanded that he clarify both the origin of the jewels found in his office and the payments received by his daughters' company. Sources from the party have indicated that the former president has the right to defend his innocence, but have added that he "continues to disappoint" and that his intervention on TVE has not been up to the explanations expected by a part of the progressive electorate.

offbeat.title: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
offbeat.summary: Also, the full trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil features a likable everyman. Coyote vs. Acme hits theaters on August 28, 2026. Resident Evil The Resident Evil film franchise has grossed over $1. 2 billion worldwide since the first film debuted in 2002, but an attempt to reboot it a few years ago floundered. Sony Pictures is trying again, this time tapping Zach Cregger—who wrote, produced, and directed last year’s Oscar-winning horror hit Weapons —for the project. The studio showed the first teaser for Cregger’s Resident Evil during CinemaCon in April and dropped the full trailer today at SDCC.

tech.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment
tech.summary: As the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr has authority over the nation’s TV, radio, and internet. But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has wielded that power to threaten broadcasters that have exercised their free speech rights to make jokes about the president. He was even able to get ABC to briefly pull Jimmy Kimmel from his late-night show. As The Verge editor-in-chief Nilay Patel has said, Carr’s mission is to “trash the First Amendment. ” We’ll be following closely as the FCC chairman attempts to erode Americans’ constitutional rights.

tech.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
tech.summary: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319. 99 (usually $399. 99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. [Product: Amazon Echo Show 21 | https://bestbuy. 7tiv. net/c/482924/614286/10014? u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww. bestbuy. com%2Fproduct%2Famazon-echo-show-21-full-hd-21-smart-display-for-home-organization-with-built-in-fire-tv-and-alexa-black%2FJ39HW6R5LZ, https://go. skimresources. com/?

tech.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
tech.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic’s faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering answers to quick questions with minimal delay. But in a blog post, the company said people immediately started using voice mode for far more than casual queries. They were using it to work “through real business problems,” which Haiku was not really designed for. That model “kept conversations quick, but not always deep.

breaking.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
breaking.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

breaking.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
breaking.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

entertainment.title: Beach Boys Call Off Remainder of Summer Concerts ‘to Recharge’ Before Resuming in Fall
entertainment.summary: The Beach Boys ‘ endless summer has run into a hitch. The touring version of the group, led by sole remaining original member Mike Love , has called off six weeks’ worth of concerts scheduled for this summer, amounting to nearly 30 dates, citing the need to “recharge” before hitting the road again in the fall. The cancellations and postponements cover the concerts that the band had scheduled from Thursday night, when they were to have played at Wolf Trap in Virginia, through Sept. 3, when they were scheduled to perform at the Colorado State Fair.

entertainment.title: ‘Avatar: Seven Havens’ Sets 2026 Release Date at Paramount+, Drops Apocalyptic First Teaser
entertainment.summary: “ Avatar: Seven Havens ” has an official premiere date at Paramount+ . And what a date it is. The new entry in the “Avatar” franchise will debut on Oct. 9 on the streaming service. That date should stick out to fans, as it was originally the date set for the premiere of the movie “Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender,” before that release was moved up to July 25 after the movie leaked online earlier this year. And unlike past “Avatar” shows, which released weekly and had time between seasons, all 26 episodes of “Seven Havens” (divided into two “books” as with the past shows) will debut all at once as a binge release.

facts.title: BBC Inside Science
facts.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

facts.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
facts.summary: 2 min read Jessica Taveau Jul 23, 2026 RELEASE 26-059 Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. Fire TV customers can easily access this content by asking Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future programming on Fire TV may include science mission launches, a test flight for rendezvous and docking with human landing systems, robotic lunar deliveries, the first crewed mission to the Moon under Artemis, and more. As always, NASA+ also remains available for free, with no ads, through the NASA app and on the agency’s website .

facts.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
facts.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

crisis.title: Help for electricity bills as crude oil hits $100
crisis.summary: The States of Guernsey said it would give Alderney Electricity £200,000 to offset islanders' bills. Jeannie Cameron, who chairs the committee, said the funds would be applied directly to customers' bills. She said: "The recent rise in global oil prices has the potential to place additional financial pressure on households and businesses at a time when many are already managing increased costs. "This temporary measure provides a level of protection against exceptional fuel price increases while ensuring that public funds are only used where they are genuinely needed. " Cameron said unlike its sister islands Guernsey and Jersey, Alderney did not have cable supplying electricity from France.

politics.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
politics.summary: Some hospitality firms will get a discount from next April but hotels and restaurants won't get a cut. By Henry Zeffman Chief political correspondent and Archie Mitchell Business reporter Pubs, social clubs and live music venues in England will be given a 20% cut to business rates from April, with new Prime Minister Andy Burnham calling it a "first step" to help the industry. It is his third policy announcement since entering No 10, and the government estimates the plan will save firms around £1,100 next year. With a cost of £100m, it will be funded by a review of tax relief on firms such as vape shops which "do not make a positive contribution to local communities", the government said.

politics.title: U.S. to Withdraw Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
politics.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times said were an attempt to intimidate the free press.

politics.title: Clint Reilly, Political Consultant Known for Feuding, Dies at 79
politics.summary: As savvy as he could be volatile, he advised Nancy Pelosi and Dianne Feinstein, ran for mayor of San Francisco and bought The San Francisco Examiner.

sports.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
sports.summary: Italy held discussions with Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before opening talks with Pep Guardiola. Italy approached Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before holding talks with Pep Guardiola. The Azzurri have been without a head coach since Gennaro Gattuso resigned in April following the country's failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup. Earlier this week, Italian Football Federation (FIGC) president Giovanni Malago said talks had been held with Guardiola, who left Manchester City this summer after 10 years, and "exceptions" could be made for his salary.

sports.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
sports.summary: AC Milan midfielder Luka Modric - who turns 41 in September - signs a one-year contract extension at the club. AC Milan midfielder Luka Modric - who turns 41 in September - has signed a one-year contract extension. The Croatian joined Milan last summer on a one-year deal with an option to extend after leaving Real Madrid. After 13 years at the Bernabeu, Modric scored two goals in 37 appearances for Milan in a season when they finished fifth in Serie A and only reached the last-16 stage of the Coppa Italia. "I am thrilled to stay at AC Milan, where you made me feel welcomed and loved from the very first day," Modric said of his new deal.

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

health.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
health.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

health.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
health.summary: Long-term exposure to road traffic noise is associated with a higher risk of Parkinson’s disease, the biggest study of its kind has found. Parkinson’s disease is a progressive disorder in which parts of the brain become damaged over time, affecting movement and balance. Existing research has found potential biological pathways that link noise exposure to Parkinson’s disease, largely due to stress responses and disrupted sleep. The researchers modelled noise exposure at the most and least exposed exterior of the residence of each participant and calculated the difference in noise levels. The magnitude of the effect was modest but consistent; at the most exposed facade, for every 11.
